text: Best thing last week was the fact that BTC barely moved despite semis-to-mag7/ kospi crashing. but equity fragility story mainly contained to equities and not spilling over into rates or crypto. BTC-nasdaq correlation was around 0.41~ The worst thing is BTC wasn't really bid that much despite huge relief. So it seems, quiet selling quiet bidding. This is a characteristic of winter. Oil at 85 despite US / Iran renewed aggression means rerouting and absorption already priced in (Thanks china for cutting demand by a metric fkton and showing US that it has a bajillion reserves of crude stored away on the mainland). Model assumptions based on escalation contained (50%) vs outright war (20% trump is a nutter and you never know if bibi can somehow bring up more dirt on him) vs deescalation (30%) which means probable chop till this finishes. Don't think we're going to the 2022 analog bc fed was hiking into the Ukraine / Russia conflict which broke the market and crypto. This time fed looks like they're just in the monitor and hold the door mode. Although, some on the fed are actively talking about hiking.

text: USDT Faces 2028 Compliance Deadline for Continued U.S. Exchange Access According to CoinDesk, Tether and other foreign stablecoin issuers may have until July 2028 to meet GENIUS Act requirements or risk becoming ineligible for listing on U.S. centralized exchanges. Compliance could require OCC registration, adherence to U.S. freeze and seizure orders, and potential changes to USDT's reserve structure. Federal regulators have yet to finalize the implementing rules. — link

text: Think we see a rotation back to better coins and eco's this week. The second half of last week was a mess and the type of action you see towards the end of onchain cleanses, traders recover from their hangover that the initial runners caused and take a step back. PA is typically slower and less appetite, although I think we see traders becoming more sensible by actually looking at narratives and not purely volume from notification bots. If the jimothy pump bundle dies then i think that'll be the end of runners for at least a few days. Cobie joined base and they still managed to fuck up within 24 hours, that chain deserves the death penalty. From a strength perspective, Cashcat performed worse than Ansem because it relied on uncertain short term interactions and endorsements. Ansems roadmap and mindset is much longer term so people are willing to step in and defend levels better, except that reduces the short term volatility since you can forecast the catalysts/development easier. If RH is the "new" eco to play then it's going to have these mass flush outs like Sol did in the early days where everyone doubts and slowly increases in consensus wave by wave. Again they have a hell of a resume but that's not going to be enough to take network effects from Sol, needs to be something much deeper. Look at RH from this perspective: we've been held hostage and fucked over by pumpfun since they changed the fee and swap mechanisms, base can't tie up their own shoelaces, sol has built good infra but seems to have given up on supporting any narrative as their finger is never on the pulse, bnb is lead by one of the biggest criminals on this planet, eth is a desert. All we're left with is hyperliquid and RH. If the RH team are genuinely willing to support narratives and builders with an external user base coming online which they help facilitate, why wouldn't the desperate gamblers enter a new casino with better odds? People are willing to take a chance on something new after all the fuckery onchains been dealing with. While there's a lot of noise from the sidelines of people who don't actually trade, the consensus I'm seeing from the smarter active crowd is that it doesn't die yet and traders will be willing to experiment. Tbh this has probably been the most "refreshing" narrative we've had since Launchcoin, where you can believe in a larger paradigm shift for the current environment. Tough to have much insight as volumes been exceptionally low the past 24 hours, another reason why I think we'll have a cleanse and return to fundamentals the next few days. Cashcats stopped bleeding for now and I think we'll see some more novel utility plays spawn as devs have had time to hone in on their products.

text: Highlight Clip Circle President responds to the stock plunging from $260 to $62 Circle President responds to the stock plunging from $260 to $62 On July 14, 2026, during an interview with FOX Business, Circle President Heath Tarbert addressed concerns regarding the company's stock price dropping from $260 to $62. He stated that Circle is playing the long game, believing that if they achieve their mission of building full-stack internet platform infrastructure, in the long run, the stock will take care of itself. In response to emerging competition from the 140-company Open USD consortium, which includes Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, and Google, Heath Tarbert expressed that USDC's network effects—backed by $73 billion in circulation and native support on 34 chains—are incredibly hard to replicate. Regarding the competitive landscape with Tether, he noted that USDC is the largest regulated stablecoin and holds the highest actual transaction volume. — link

text: Highlight Clip Jeff Yan: Crypto's biggest problem is the lack of top entrepreneurial talent Jeff Yan: Crypto's biggest problem is the lack of top entrepreneurial talent On July 9, 2026, Hyperliquid co-founder Jeff Yan stated in an interview on the VALR podcast that a fair criticism of the current crypto and fintech sectors is their inability to attract enough of the highest quality entrepreneurial talent. He believes that amidst the AI boom and the social pressure to pursue prestige, the smartest young people often do not know what they could do to make the most impact, resulting in very few choosing to enter the space. Jeff Yan expressed that rebuilding financial engineering from the ground up based on first principles and translating academic concepts into scalable market designs is an incredible undertaking. He urged the younger generation not to take things at surface value, but to identify the real problems the world faces and participate in the incredible renaissance of on-chain finance happening right now. — link
